note.ko: 2017년 대선 후보 시절 마크롱은 원전 비중을 75%에서 50%로 축소하고 페센하임 원전을 폐쇄하는 계획을 지지했으나, 2022년 2월에는 신규 EPR2 원전 6기 건설과 추가 8기 검토를 발표하며 '원자력 부흥'으로 입장을 뒤집었다.
note.en: In 2017 Macron endorsed cutting nuclear power's share from 75% to 50% and closing the Fessenheim plant, but in February 2022 he reversed course, announcing six new EPR2 reactors and a nuclear renaissance.

statements.text: As a 2017 candidate, Macron endorsed the plan to cut nuclear power's share of French electricity to 50% (from ~75%) by around 2025 and to close the Fessenheim plant.
In February 2022 Macron announced France would build six new EPR2 nuclear reactors, with eight more under consideration — a nuclear 'renaissance'.

note.ko: 마크롱은 2023년 3월~4월 49.3조를 동원해 정년을 62세에서 64세로 올리는 연금개혁을 강행 처리했으나, 2025년 10월 르코르뉴 총리를 통해 이 개혁의 시행을 2027년 대선 이후로 유예하기로 하며 입장을 뒤집었다.
note.en: Macron forced through the pension reform raising the retirement age from 62 to 64 via Article 49.3 in March-April 2023, then reversed course in October-November 2025 by backing PM Lecornu's suspension of that reform until after the 2027 election.

statements.text: Macron's government invoked Article 49.3 to force the pension reform raising the retirement age from 62 to 64 through parliament without a final vote.
Macron's plan to raise France's retirement age from 62 to 64 was enacted into law in April 2023, a central plank of his second-term agenda.
PM Lecornu, with Macron's backing, announced suspension of the 2023 pension reform — effectively freezing the retirement age at 62 years and nine months rather than 64.
France's National Assembly voted to suspend the flagship pension reform law until after the 2027 presidential election.

note.ko: 2024년 6월 마크롱은 조기 총선을 단행하며 이를 안정을 위한 결정으로 정당화했으나, 2024년 12월 31일에는 그 결정이 오히려 '더 큰 불안정'을 초래했다고 인정하며 입장을 뒤집었다.
note.en: Macron justified calling snap elections in June 2024 as a stabilizing move, but by December 31, 2024 he reversed course, admitting the decision had actually caused 'more instability'.

statements.text: After the National Rally topped the June 2024 European elections, Macron dissolved the National Assembly and called snap legislative elections for 30 June and 7 July 2024.
Macron acknowledged that his decision to call the snap legislative elections had caused 'more instability' than it resolved.
I do not believe for a single second in reforms by decree; deep reforms cannot be imposed on the country by ordinance.
Macron ran in 2017 as 'neither right nor left' (ni de droite ni de gauche), positioning himself as a centrist beyond the traditional divide.

note.ko: 2022년 6월에는 러시아를 모욕하지 말고 외교적 출구를 마련해야 한다는 유화적 입장을 보였으나, 2024년 2월부터는 지상군 파견 가능성을 배제하지 않고 러시아가 승리할 수 없도록 하겠다는 더 강경한 입장으로 전환했다.
note.en: In June 2022 Macron took a conciliatory stance saying Russia should not be humiliated and a diplomatic exit should be preserved, but by February 2024 he shifted to a tougher position, refusing to rule out sending troops and vowing Russia must not win.

statements.text: We must not humiliate Russia so that the day the fighting stops, we can build a way out through diplomatic channels.
There's no consensus today to send troops in an official manner, but in terms of dynamics, nothing can be ruled out; we will do everything needed so Russia cannot win the war.
Macron reaffirmed that sending Western troops to Ukraine could not be ruled out and that he maintained deliberate 'strategic ambiguity' toward Russia.

note.ko: 2019년 11월 나토를 뇌사 상태라고 표현했고, 2023년 5월에도 같은 표현을 인용하며 푸틴이 그 뇌사 상태의 나토를 충격요법으로 되살렸다고 언급해 입장이 일관되게 유지됨.
note.en: Macron called NATO brain-dead in November 2019, and in May 2023 he referenced that same phrase, saying Putin revived the brain-dead NATO with shock therapy, showing a consistent position rather than a reversal.

statements.text: We are experiencing the brain death of NATO.
Vladimir Putin has revived the 'brain-dead' NATO with the worst of electroshocks.
I have decided that France will recognize the State of Palestine, with a solemn announcement before the UN General Assembly in September.

note.ko: 2018년 두 발언 모두 부의 재분배보다 생산과 투자 촉진을 우선시하는 입장으로 일관되며(부유세 폐지 및 부동산세로 대체 포함), 명확한 입장 반전은 없음.
note.en: Both 2018 statements consistently prioritize wealth creation and investment over redistribution, including the replacement of the wealth tax (ISF) with a narrower real-estate tax (IFI), showing no clear reversal.

statements.text: It's all well and good to want to spread wealth, but you first need to produce, to create wealth before redistributing.
Macron delivered on his pledge to abolish France's Solidarity Tax on Wealth (ISF), replacing it with a narrower real-estate wealth tax (IFI) to encourage investment.
Defending the December 2023 immigration law, Macron said the bill is 'what the French wanted' and denied it echoed the far right.
